Thank you for the comments. I rebased the patch and made the suggested improvements to the tests.

I added your 'Signed-off-by' back to the patch, but I don't know how to restore your name as author. Is there a way I can do this in the commit message or when submitting the patch?

I do want credit to go where it should.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+2

As for submitting a patch with a different Author, you can use:

 git commit --author "Author Name <joe.author@email.com>" 

But it is possible that Gerrit doesn't allow you to commit this without special permissions. Normally, if you import a patch via "git cherry-pick" or "git am" it should preserve the author info.

Patch Set 8:

James, it wouldn't hurt for you to also mark this patch +1, even though you submitted it, since I'm not sure if my +2 is ignored because I'm the owner of this change.

Sarah or Saurabh, please review.

parents.subject: LU-7593 target: umount vs tgt_last_rcvd_update deadlock

commit.subject: LU-7813 tests: clean up ost-pools.sh
commit.message:
LU-7813 tests: clean up ost-pools.sh

Clean up the tests in ost-pools.sh to drop archaic use of
"lfs getstripe -v" that parses the output text in favour of
using options for "lfs getstripe -c" for OST count.

Add the check for newly-created dir/file being in the pool
into create_dir() and create_file().

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=ost-pools

Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ib2df663a62f89df48a70d07702b41f05f0194ef9
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18889
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Saurabh Tandan <saurabh.tandan@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
